revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[bs:Hoori]][[en:Hoori]][[fa:هوری (اسطوره ژاپن)]][[fr:Hoori]][[hr:Hoori]][[it:Hoori]][[ja:ホオリ]][[ko:호오리]][[mni:ꯍꯨꯔꯤ]][[nl:Ho-ori]][[no:Hoori]][[pl:Hoori]][[pt:Yamasatihiko]][[ru:Хоори]][[sh:Hoori]][[simple:Hoori]][[sr:Хоори]][[sv:Hoori]][[tr:Hoori]][[uk:Хоорі]][[vi:Hoori]][[zh:火折尊]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Hikohohodemi otokowa.png|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|occupation={{ill|monarch|qid=Q116|1=Monarch}}|title={{ill|Chiten no kimi|qid=Q11554403|1=Chiten no kimi}}|spouse={{ill|Tamayori-bime|qid=Q3082751|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}; {{ill|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}}|children={{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|qid=Q1373935|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}|father={{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}|mother={{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}|relatives={{ill|Hoderi|qid=Q2716491|1=Hoderi}}; {{ill|Honoakari|qid=Q60673696|1=Honoakari}}; {{ill|Honosusori|qid=Q10731395|1=Hosuseri}}|organization={{ill|Chijin Godai|qid=Q18337308|1=Five Generations of Earth Deities}}; {{ill|Three generations of Hyuga|qid=Q56347075|1=Three generations of Hyuga}}; {{ill|Yamasachihiko and Umisachihiko|qid=Q11468336|1=Umisachi and Yamasachi}}|native_name=彦火火出見|resting_place={{ill|Takaya Sanryō|qid=Q11669273|1=Takaya Sanryō}}}}
{{No footnotes|date=September 2023}}{{Short description|Figure in Japanese mythology}} {{Other uses}}
{{Infobox deity | type = Shinto | father = [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] | mother = [[:en:Konohana Sakuya|Konohana Sakuya]] | image =Hikohohodemi otokowa.png; File:Hikohohodemi otokowa.png| caption = Hikohohodemi otokowa | children = [[Ugayafukiaezu]] | consort ={{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}}; {{ill|Tamayori-bime|qid=Q3082751|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}; {{ill|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|1=Toyotama-hime}}| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}} | parents ={{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Konohananosakuya-bime|qid=Q1781862|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}| offspring = {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|qid=Q1373935|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} | member_of = {{ill|Chijin Godai|qid=Q18337308|1=Five Generations of Earth Deities}}; {{ill|Three generations of Hyuga|qid=Q56347075|1=Three generations of Hyuga}}; {{ill|Yamasachihiko and Umisachihiko|qid=Q11468336|1=Umisachi and Yamasachi}} | equivalent5_type = Japanese | equivalent5 = {{ill|Cintāmaṇicakra|qid=Q11446712|1=Cintāmaṇicakra}}; {{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} }}{{Nihongo|'''Hoori'''|火折尊|Hoori no Mikoto}}, also known as {{Nihongo|'''Hikohohodemi no Mikoto'''|彦火火出見尊}}, is a figure in [[Japanese mythology]], the third and youngest son of {{lang|ja-Latn|[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]|italic=no}} and the blossom princess {{lang|ja-Latn|[[Konohanasakuya-hime]]|italic=no}}. He is one of the ancestors of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperors of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}} as the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}. He is also known as {{Nihongo||山幸彦|Yamasachi-hiko}}.
==Mythology== Hoori's legend is told in both the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''. Hoori was a hunter, and he had an argument with his brother {{ill|Hoderi|en|Hoderi|qid=Q2716491|lt=Hoderi|1=Hoderi}}, a fisherman, over a fish-hook that Hoori had forced his elder brother to lend him and had lost. Hoderi claimed that Hoori should give back the fish-hook, for he refused to accept another one (due to the belief that each tool is animated and hence unique). Hoori then descended to the bottom of the sea to search, but was unable to find it. Instead, he found {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}}, the daughter of the sea god, [[Ryūjin]]. The sea god helped Hoori find Hoderi's lost hook, and Hoori later married Toyotama-hime.
Hoori lived with his wife in a palace under the sea for three years, but after that Hoori became home-sick and wished to return to his own country. His brother forgave him after he returned the hook, and Toyotama-hime gave birth to a son named [[Ugayafukiaezu]]. During the time when Toyotama-hime was giving birth to her child, she had Hoori swear not to attempt to see her real figure. But he broke his promise and discovered her true form was a {{ill|Japanese dragon|en|Japanese dragon|qid=Q2366503|lt=dragon|1=Japanese dragon}} (specifically a ''{{ill|Wani (dragon)|en|Wani (dragon)|qid=Q20049265|lt=wani|1=Wani (dragon)}}''). She was ashamed and returned to her father, never to return. Ugayafukiaezu married Toyotama-hime's sister, Tamayori-hime, who brought him up, and she gave birth to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, who was the first {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. Hoori reigned in {{ill|Takachiho, Miyazaki|en|Takachiho, Miyazaki|qid=Q1350939|lt=Takachiho|1=Takachiho, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|qid=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province|1=Hyūga Province}} for 560 years.
Hoori is often associated with both his parents and his wife. He is worshiped mainly as a god of {{ill|Cereal|en|Cereal|qid=Q12117|lt=Cereal|1=Cereal}}s or {{ill|Grain|en|Grain|qid=Q2995529|lt=Grain|1=Grain}}. In Japanese mythology, it was said that the {{Nihongo||Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|1=Fire (wuxing)|ja|Fire (火)|1=Fire (wuxing)|lt=Fire (火)|2=uk|3=Вогонь (У-сін)|4=bcl|5=Kalayo (wuxing)|6=pt|7=Fogo (astrologia)|8=vi|9=Hỏa (Ngũ hành)|10=ar|11=النار (وو شينغ)|lt=Fire (火)|lt=Fire (火)|ho}} part of his name meant fire, but etymologically, it is a different character pronounced {{Nihongo||穂|ho}}, which refers to crops, particularly rice. {{Nihongo||折り|Ori|extra=''to bend''}} indicates a crop that is so rich, it bends under its own weight. Another name for him, Hohodemi, means ''many harvests''.
==Genealogy== Hoori is part of the [[Tenson kōrin|Three generations of Hyuga]], a period between [[Tenson kōrin]] and [[Jimmu's Eastern Expedition]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-08-04 |title=みやざきの神話と伝承101：概説 |url=https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |access-date=2022-06-12 |website= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210804121336/https://www.pref.miyazaki.lg.jp/contents/org/chiiki/seikatu/miyazaki101/shinwa_densho/outline.html |archive-date=4 August 2021 |url-status=dead}}</ref> {{Three_generations_of_Hyuga}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
==External links== *{{Wikisourcelang-inline|en|The Japanese Fairy Book/The Happy Hunter and the Skilful Fisher|The Happy Hunter and the Skilful Fisher}} *''Hoderi and Hoori'' from ''Myths and Legends of Japan'' by F. Hadland Davis. {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20050130083531/http://www.personal.psu.edu/users/a/c/acj2/hoori.html |date=January 30, 2005 }}
